content_summary: This document provides a comprehensive primer on Goldman Sachs' two FX valuation models—GSDEER and GSFEER—explaining their theoretical foundations, methodologies, current signals, and historical performance. It covers the GSDEER model as a purchasing power parity-based approach for long-term fair value anchoring, the GSFEER model as a more cyclical framework based on current account imbalances, optimal weighting of both models across different time horizons, convergence rates to fair value across currencies, and specific currency observations highlighting undervalued currencies like AUD and KRW versus overvalued ones.
